violations.description: Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Open display case 46F at back to 55F in salsa. -Ambient temperature at front single door 47. -2 door glass sour cream 61F. -Single door (tea) ambient 52F, sauce 50F. -Make table top 42F-45F, bottom 42F. -Small sauce refrigerator Queso 45F, alfredo 43, bottom butter 40. *Facility has a morning and evening temp log that showed good opening and closing temperatures-all under 41F. The open face display appeared to be in defrost at time of arrival. Facility also had a busy lunch and stocked multiple items today. ***Left with instructions to cool products ASAP and monitor units for recovery.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Asian sauce not date marked.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -Seasoned/scampi butter dated 4/24.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Residue at oven handle. -Debris at interior of table top air fryer/oven.
Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. -Quat test strips on available for testing sani buckets.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. -Sani bucket filled at bar hand sink. *Corrected on-site, redirected staff to fill at bar prep sink.
